title: carex subdivulsa
intro: Carex subdivulsa, also known as the blue-green sedge, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae and is native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help alleviate symptoms of wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Carex subdivulsa include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds due to its expectorant properties. Carex subdivulsa can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and salves, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
